text:
Tom Cruise’s appearance at the2024 Olympicsclosing ceremony became one of the big talking points of the event, with the actor making a special and dramatic appearanceto collect the Olympic flag and deliver it to its next destination of Los Angeles. While the production, which included pre-recorded footage of Cruise leaving Paris by plane and skydiving down to the Hollywood sign, seemed like the kind of big budget stunt that the actor’sMission: Impossiblemovies have become known for,Cruise himself has a surprising pay packet for his appearance. Absolutely nothing.

While Cruise has been known as one of the highest paid actors in the business, it seems that he had one simple condition to do the whole thing for free – he wanted to do his own stunts. This is hardly surprising considering the actor’s history of throwing himself off buildings, mountains, out of planes, and fighting on top of moving vehicles. In comparison, his Olympics stunt was a little on the tame side.

The 8th entry in the long running Mission Impossible franchise. Starring Tom Cruise and Vanessa Kirby.

While reflecting on how Cruise’s generous offer came about, president of the LA28 committee, Casey Wasserman, shared how everything came together. When it became apparent that a 15-minute spectacle was required for the event, producer Ben Winston came up with the idea of contacting Cruise about the possibility of appearing in part of the show. Wasserman said:

“The best part of the story is we pitched on a Zoom, and the original idea was a person in the stadium as a stunt double. We're like, 'Well, there's no way we're getting this. We're going to get four hours of filming time. We'll do the thing with the Hollywood sign, he'll hand the thing off, and he's done. Maybe we'll get the other stuff and the rest will be just a stunt double.' About five minutes into the presentation, [Cruise] goes, 'I'm in. But I'm only doing it if I get to do everything.’”

Even after Cruise had agreed to take part in the production, Wasserman still had doubts that he would be able to complete everything himself considering the schedule they needed to stick to. This was also happening around the time that the actor was in the U.K. filming his latestMission: Impossiblemovie. However, Cruise’s commitment was above and beyond.

"He finished filming Mission: Impossible at 6 p.m. in London, got right on a plane," Wasserman recalled. "He landed in L.A. at 4 a.m. and filmed the scene where he pulls onto a military plane. In L.A., he does two jumps out of the [plane]. He didn't like the first one, so he did a second jump. Then he helicoptered from Palmdale to the Hollywood sign, filmed from 1 until 5, helicoptered to Burbank Airport, and flew back to London."

From Vanilla Sky to Mission: Impossible 7, Tom Cruise dove headfirst into some of the most imaginative sci-fi movies, some better than others.

It seems that when Tom Cruise says he will do something, then he comes through no matter what. As a man now in his 60s, the actor’s work ethic is something that can never be questioned, as his commitment to the craft is in a league of its own. Following his Olympics spectacle,all eyes are now on his eighthMission: Impossibleouting, which is expected to arrive in cinemas on May 23, 2025.

We want to hear from you! Share your opinions in the thread below and remember to keep it respectful.

title: Tom Cruise's Shocking Olympics Paycheck Happened for One Reason
description: Tom Cruise saw out the 2024 Olympics in style, but how much did he earn from it?
